title: Ensure double encryption is used for Azure Data Box in  high-security environments

input_row.title: Ensure double encryption is used for Azure Data Box in  high-security environments
input_row.description: Enabling double encryption on Azure Data Box applies an additional layer of encryption to safeguard data during physical transfer. This approach enhances confidentiality and integrity, ensuring that sensitive information remains secure against unauthorized access if the device is lost, stolen, or intercepted.
input_row.rationale: Double encryption ensures strong security for high-risk or regulated environments where data protection is critical. It enhances defense-in-depth and minimizes the risk of exposure, even during physical compromise in transit.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Policy If referencing a digital copy of this Benchmark, clicking a Policy ID will open a link to the associated Policy definition in Azure. If referencing a printed copy, you can search Policy IDs from this URL: https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_Azure_Policy/PolicyMenuBlade/~/Definitions • Policy ID: c349d81b-9985-44ae-a8da-ff98d108ede8 • Name: 'Azure Data Box jobs should enable double encryption for data at rest on the device'
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal When creating a new Azure Data Box order, on the Security page, under Double encryption, check the box next to Enable double encryption for the order.
input_row.impact: Double encryption with Azure Data Box is available at no additional cost; however, enabling it may increase order processing and data copy times.
input_row.default_value: Double encryption is disabled by default on Azure Data Box orders.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Navigate to AWS Management Console.** Go to the AWS Management Console and sign in.
2. **Check for Data Box Orders.** In the AWS Console, search for and navigate to the "AWS DataBox" service.
3. **Review Order Security Settings.** Check current Azure Data Box orders you may have set up and look for a section on encryption settings, specifically the Double Encryption option.
4. **Expected Secure Configuration**: Ensure that the "Enable double encryption for the order" setting is checked for any Data Box orders in high-security environments.
5. **Confirm Compliance.** Document whether the double encryption is enabled. If it is not enabled, it needs to be addressed to align with the security standard as per your organizational policies. 

Please note that since this is based on Azure's security requirements regarding Data Box and does not directly translate to an AWS environment, the steps may not lead to relevant results in AWS, and it demonstrates the need for understanding service equivalents across platforms.
